channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump announces more nominations, including Devin Nunes, Troy Edgar and Bill White
item.description: Trump nominated Truth Social CEO Devin Nunes as chairman of his Intelligence Advisory Board, Troy Edgar as deputy secretary of Homeland Security and Bill White as ambassador to Belgium.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, close allies attend Army-Navy rivalry game
item.description: As national concerns about the drones spotted on the East Coast continue to mount, President-elect Trump is attending the Army-Navy game.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken says American officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that led the overthrow of Bashar Assad’s government but is designated a foreign terrorist organization by the United States and others.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump brings political excitement to Army-Navy game
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance will attend the annual Army-Navy game in Landover, Maryland, on Saturday, and they are bringing some guests who are sure to stir debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats need new playbook to confront Trump, Kamala Harris pollster tells party
item.description: A top pollster to Vice President Kamala Harris says the Democrats need a new playbook to politically confront Donald Trump as he returns to the White House, and that they should focus on high prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump wants to turn the clock on daylight saving time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump wants to turn the lights out on daylight saving time. In a post on his social media site Friday, Trump said his party would try to end the practice when he returns to office.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi hospitalized in Luxembourg after sustaining injury from fall on stairs
item.description: Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) was hospitalized for evaluation in Luxembourg after falling on a set of stairs and sustaining an injury during a trip with a congressional delegation.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: A nonprofit leader, a social worker: Here are the stories of the people on Biden's clemency list
item.description: Among the roughly 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted or who were pardoned by President Joe Biden are a nonprofit leader who supports at-risk youth, a social worker who fosters animals and a postdoctoral researcher One woman who received a pardon said she wanted people with drug addiction to
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House releases national strategy to combat Islamophobia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Thursday announced what it called the first-ever national strategy to counter Islamophobia, detailing more than 100 steps federal officials are taking to curb hate, violence, bias and discrimination against Muslims and Arab Americans.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: Asked at a Chinese Foreign Ministry briefing on Thursday about Trump's invitation, spokesperson Mao Ning responded: “I have nothing to share at present.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: READ: Here is Biden's full list of clemencies
item.description: President Biden early Thursday announced that he is pardoning 39 people and commuting the sentences of nearly 1,500 others, setting a new daily record for clemency with a focus on those who were under home confinement during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Balluck, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis City Council overrides mayor’s veto of 2025 budget
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council enacted its 2025 budget on Thursday, after overturning a veto by Mayor Jacob Frey.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump mass deportation threats infuriate immigrant advocates
item.description: President-elect Trump’s pledge to enact mass deportations and end birthright citizenship, potentially affecting millions of U.S. citizens, is raising alarm bells among immigrant communities who fear an overzealous Trump administration could destroy countless families.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences for 1,500 people, the largest act of clemency in a day
item.description: The 1,500 people had been serving long prison sentences that would have been shorter under today's laws and practices. Three Minnesota women are among those to get their federal convictions pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith and Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is back in his hometown of New York where he's been honored as Time magazine’s Person of the Year and will ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's withdrawn nominees latest in a long line of Cabinet members who never were
item.description: Many presidents face failed Cabinet picks so when former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration for President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet, it wasn't unprecedented, though Trump's style has been.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NJ bill looks to make doxxing a crime
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - When someone is doxxed, it can be more than just a leak of their information; it can rob someone of their well-being, physically and mentally.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: As session nears, status of a Minnesota lawmaker facing criminal charges remains a flashpoint
item.description: Sen. Nicole Mitchell has upcoming court hearings in a burglary charge ahead of a trial that would start soon after 2025 Legislature gavels in. She’s resisting calls to step down, and a departure would leave the Senate knotted up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk warns Republicans against standing in Trump's way — or his
item.description: Donald Trump’s second term comes with the specter of the world’s richest man serving as his political enforcer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, has promised to deliver a massive reform of a bureau he sees as among the agencies that have “weaponized the government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
